name: Sai Teja Ragula

summary: AI Engineer with 5 plus years of software development experience and deep hands-on expertise building production AI applications using Large Language Models. Currently at Frontier Communications in Dallas building LLM-powered features on Google Cloud Platform with Vertex AI and Gemini. Skilled in multi-model orchestration, RAG pipelines, agentic workflows, and prompt engineering. Strong full stack background with Java Spring Boot backend and React TypeScript frontend. Experienced deploying AI systems on GCP and AWS with Kubernetes and Docker. IBM certified in Prompt Engineering. Oracle AI Foundations certified. AWS Solutions Architect certified.

aiAndLLMs: Multi-Model Orchestration — routing queries to the right model by complexity
Prompt Engineering — chain-of-thought, few-shot, system prompts, output formatting
RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) — Pinecone, embeddings, semantic search
Agentic Workflows — tool use, function calling, autonomous task pipelines
AI Agents — multi-step reasoning, memory, planning
cloudAndMLOps: GCP — Vertex AI, Cloud Run, Cloud Storage, BigQuery

experience.title: AI/Software Engineer
experience.description: Frontier is a major US telecom provider. I work on their enterprise AI team building LLM-powered features on GCP.
highlights: Integrated Vertex AI and Gemini 1.5 into enterprise telecom applications to automate customer-facing workflows that previously required human agents

Built a multi-model orchestration layer that routes incoming requests to Gemini Flash for simple queries and Gemini Pro for complex reasoning — reduced average response latency by 40 percent while cutting cost
Implemented NLP-based SMS integration using LLM classification to automatically categorize and respond to customer text messages at scale
Designed and built RAG pipelines — chunked internal documentation, embedded with OpenAI text-embedding-3-small, stored in Pinecone, and wired to Gemini for context-aware answers
Engineered production prompt templates with chain-of-thought reasoning, few-shot examples, and output format constraints for consistent, reliable LLM outputs
Built agentic workflows using function calling where the AI can query internal APIs, look up account info, and take actions on behalf of customers
Developed full stack features with Java Spring Boot REST APIs and React TypeScript frontend — end to end ownership
Deployed AI-integrated microservices on GCP Cloud Run with Docker and Kubernetes, handling autoscaling under variable load
Built event-driven AI processing pipelines using Apache Kafka — real-time stream processing for customer interaction data
Achieved 90 percent plus code coverage through unit and integration testing of AI integration layers using JUnit and Mockito

experience.title: Software Engineer
experience.description: Worked as a backend engineer at Wipro on enterprise Java applications.
highlights: Developed backend microservices using Java Spring Boot for enterprise B2B applications
Built and documented RESTful APIs consumed by frontend teams and third-party integrators
Implemented automated data processing workflows using Apache Kafka event streaming
Migrated legacy monolith components to containerized microservices using Docker and deployed via CI/CD pipelines on cloud infrastructure

experience.title: Software Engineer
experience.description: Full stack developer building client-facing web applications.
highlights: Built full stack web applications using Java Spring Boot backend and React JavaScript frontend
Developed real-time data processing systems using Apache Kafka for event-driven architecture
Implemented RESTful APIs and handled frontend-backend integration across the complete feature lifecycle
Deployed containerized applications on cloud platforms and maintained CI/CD pipelines

projects.name: Wealthix — AI-Powered Financial Intelligence Platform
projects.description: End-to-end AI financial platform. Built a Hybrid Router pattern that dynamically selects between Gemini 1.5 and Claude based on query type — fast financial lookups go to Gemini Flash, complex reasoning like tax optimization goes to Claude. Implemented a full RAG pipeline: PDF financial documents ingested, chunked, embedded with OpenAI embeddings, stored in Pinecone, retrieved by semantic similarity at query time. Deployed on AWS with load balancing and monitoring.
highlights: Multi-model orchestration: cost-optimized routing between Gemini Flash and Claude based on prompt complexity score
RAG pipeline end to end: ingest, chunk, embed, upsert, retrieve, inject into LLM context
Plaid integration for real bank account data — AI reasons over actual transaction history
Stripe payment integration with AI-generated invoice summaries

projects.name: AI Financial Co-Pilot — Automated Dispute Engine
projects.description: Agentic AI system for automated financial dispute resolution. The AI reads dispute documents using Claude 3.5, extracts key claims, cross-references transaction records, drafts resolution letters, and routes edge cases to human review. Built prompt engineering pipelines with structured output constraints so Claude always returns valid JSON that maps to dispute categories.
highlights: Claude 3.5 for document analysis — structured output with JSON schema enforcement
Agentic routing: AI decides whether to auto-resolve or escalate to human
Prompt pipeline with few-shot examples for consistent dispute classification
Real-time dashboard showing dispute status, AI confidence scores, and resolution timeline

projects.name: AI Call Screener — Voice AI Agent
projects.description: Real-time voice AI agent that intercepts recruiter phone calls and answers as me. Twilio Media Streams provides μ-law 8kHz audio via WebSocket. Deepgram nova-3 does streaming STT. Claude claude-sonnet-4-6 reasons from my resume context with RAG (Pinecone) for self-improvement after each call. ElevenLabs TTS generates the voice response. The system detects interruptions using AtomicReference and stops mid-sentence — exactly like a real person would. Conference bridge architecture enables seamless live call handoff between AI and human.
highlights: Real-time audio pipeline: Twilio WebSocket → Deepgram STT → Claude reasoning → ElevenLabs TTS → Twilio
Interrupt detection: AtomicReference tracks mid-speech caller interruptions, AI stops at sentence boundary
Conference bridge: Twilio conference rooms allow seamless AI ↔ human call handoff with no audio gap
Self-training: each call transcript ingested into Pinecone RAG so AI improves over time
Java virtual threads for non-blocking AI pipeline — each call runs concurrently without blocking

Bachelor of Technology in Computer Science

commonInterviewAnswers.tellMeAboutYourself: I am Sai Teja Ragula, an AI Engineer based in Dallas. I have 5 plus years of software development experience and for the past 3 years I have been focused specifically on building production LLM applications. I currently work at Frontier Communications where I integrate Vertex AI and Gemini into enterprise telecom workflows. My stack is Java Spring Boot on the backend, React TypeScript on the frontend, and GCP for cloud. I have built RAG pipelines, multi-model orchestration systems, and agentic AI workflows in production. I am currently looking for an AI or senior software engineering role where I can go deeper into applied LLM systems.
commonInterviewAnswers.whyLeavingCurrentJob: I have learned a lot at Frontier — shipping LLM features at enterprise scale has been great experience. I am now looking for a role that is even more AI-focused, ideally where AI is core to the product rather than a feature layer on top of an existing system.
commonInterviewAnswers.biggestStrength: I can take an AI concept from prototype to production end to end. I understand the LLM layer — prompting, RAG, orchestration — and I also understand the engineering underneath — APIs, distributed systems, cloud deployment. A lot of people are good at one or the other. I am comfortable across the full stack.
commonInterviewAnswers.biggestWeakness: I sometimes go deep into a problem when a simpler solution would have been fine. I am getting better at stepping back and asking what the minimum viable approach is before over-engineering.
commonInterviewAnswers.whereDoYouSeeYourselfIn5Years: Building AI systems at a larger scale — ideally as a lead or principal engineer on a team where AI is central to the business. I want to be the person who sets the architecture for how LLMs are integrated across a product, not just implementing features one at a time.
commonInterviewAnswers.salaryExpectation: I am looking at 65 to 70 dollars per hour on W2 or C2C.
commonInterviewAnswers.whyShouldWeHireYou: Because I have actually shipped LLM applications in production, not just built demos. I know what breaks — prompt injection, hallucination, latency, cost at scale — and I know how to handle it. I bring both the AI expertise and the full stack engineering to build things that actually work in production.
